items.headline: Sam Malinski's poise on display for Colorado in 2026 playoffs
items.description:
DENVER -- Sam Malinski sat at his stall, his bottom lip bloodied from a wayward puck he took to the mouth during Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round, the same game he scored the first Stanley Cup Playoff goal of his career.

items.headline: Noah Cates injury status update May 6 2026
items.description:
Noah Cates will miss the rest of the Eastern Conference Second Round for the Philadelphia Flyers with a lower-body injury, coach Rick Tocchet said Wednesday.
Cates, a center, played 20:53 in a 3-2 loss at the Carolina Hurricanes on Monday. He has four points (one goal, three assists) in eight

items.headline: Brock Faber's 2-way game key for Minnesota in West 2nd Round
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Brock Faber's two-way game is as important to the Minnesota Wild right now as it has ever been with the hole they're in after losing the first two games to the Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Second Round.

items.description: NHL.com's fantasy staff continues to cover the latest trends and storylines in the League through the lens of NHL EDGE puck and player tracker stats. Today, we provide the underlying metrics behind Nathan MacKinnon of the Colorado Avalanche.

items.headline: Selke Trophy finalists for best defensive forward of 2025-26 announced by NHL
items.description:
Anthony Cirelli, Brock Nelson and Nick Suzuki were named finalists for the Frank J. Selke Trophy on Wednesday.
The winner of the award, which is given annually to the forward voted best to excel in the defensive aspects of the game and the NHL as voted on by the Professional

items.headline: NHL At The Rink podcast Carolina Hurricanes harder to play in playoffs
items.description: The Carolina Hurricanes are more hardened, experienced and mentally tougher in the Stanley Cup Playoffs this season than in previous runs, including three that ended in the Eastern Conference Final since Rod Brind'Amour became coach in 2018-19, according to analyst Tripp Tracy.

items.headline: King Clancy 2026 nominee essay: Alexander Wennberg
items.description: Editor's Note: The King Clancy Memorial Trophy is presented annually to the NHL player who best exemplifies leadership qualities on and off the ice and has made a noteworthy humanitarian contribution in his community. Each NHL club nominates a player to be considered for this recognition, and each nominee has

items.headline: Live NHL Draft Lottery show in real time compelling super-exciting
items.description:
SECAUCUS, N.J. -- The NHL Draft Lottery has always seemed to carry the ingredients of the sport itself, from hope, chance and the belief that one moment could bend the future of a franchise.
The Toronto Maple Leafs gained that opportunity after winning the 2026 version at NHL Network studios

items.headline: Toronto Maple Leafs have huge opportunity after winning Draft Lottery
items.description:
TORONTO -- Congratulations on your new jobs, Mats Sundin and John Chayka.
In lieu of a nice bottle of wine, how does the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NHL Draft sound as a welcoming gift?

items.headline: Mock 2026 Draft: Gavin McKenna to Toronto
items.description:
Following the NHL Draft Lottery on Tuesday, NHL.com deputy managing editor Adam Kimelman and senior draft writer Mike G. Morreale make their predictions for how the first 16 selections of the 2026 Upper Deck NHL Draft could turn out.

items.headline: NHL Power Players youth advisory board applications now being accepted 2026
items.description: NEW YORK/TORONTO -- For the eighth straight year, the National Hockey League (NHL®) will accept applications for NHL Power Players™, a youth advisory board which brings together young hockey fans, ages 13-17, to provide input to the NHL on hockey-related topics important to their generation.
